name: Dead Code Detector
description: Finds unused exports, unreachable functions, orphaned files and stale feature flags, verifies each candidate to avoid false positives, and produces a safe, reviewable removal plan.
author: ClaudeStudio Code Quality Pack

inputs.label: Known entry points
inputs.help: Comma-separated list of entry files / exported public API surfaces that must be treated as 'used' (e.g. src/main.ts, public API, CLI commands).

inputs.label: Count test files as usage
inputs.help: If true, a symbol referenced only by tests still counts as used.

steps.title: Collect candidates
steps.prompt: Analyze {{target_dir}}. Build a symbol/reference graph from the entry points {{entrypoints}} (plus conventional entry points if none given). Identify candidate dead code: exported functions/classes/constants with no references, unreachable private functions, files imported nowhere, and feature flags or config keys that are defined but never read. Honor include_tests = {{include_tests}} when deciding whether test-only references count.

steps.title: Verify candidates
steps.prompt: For each candidate, reduce false positives: check for dynamic usage (reflection, string-based imports, dependency injection, route registration, framework conventions), public API exposure, re-exports, and references from config or build files. Classify each as 'confirmed dead', 'likely dead (needs review)', or 'keep (false positive)' with the evidence.

steps.title: Removal plan
steps.prompt: Write a Markdown report: 'Summary' (counts and estimated lines removable), 'Confirmed Dead Code' (file, symbol, evidence), 'Needs Human Review', and a 'Safe Removal Plan' ordered so each removal compiles independently. Do not delete anything; only recommend. Note any change that would alter the public API.
